item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only issue with this is the line "All civilians affected by SCP-1085 are to be treated as well as possible, though no confirmed survivors have as yet been located."

This seems wildly out of character for the foundation, especially if the exposed civilians are essentially going to die, why waste resources or even consider treating them well? Termination seems more in tone with their practice and almost seems more merciful.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Alright, new attempt at crosslink.

Timer Removed.
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show whatever
- Hide whatever

Suggesting adding another addendum.

Addendum-1085-C SCP-1038 has currently been suggested for crosstesting by Site-38 researchers, due to the possibility of learning more about other versions of SCP-1085. Suggestion currently pending approval by Site 38 Dirctor Jones.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: In general, it would help to let others know of your justification of a crosslink. That way, others can properly evaluate the strength of the crosslink. You may know, but that does not mean others can infer without any doubt whatsoever.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Yea, me neither.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I don't quite understand the link here

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show whatever
- Hide whatever

Change this:

One individual (D-5656) was exposed to all four weeks of SCP-1085 over the course of four days, developing symptoms along the expected schedule.

To this:

One individual (D-108) was exposed to all four weeks of SCP-1085 over the course of four days, developing symptoms along the expected schedule.

New Crosslink Suggested Below.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry it took me so long to get to this, because it's one of the best media-based SCPs.

Current research suggests that the entity portraying ███████ ████████ in SCP-1085 may be an actual organism rather than a hallucination on the parts of affected individuals.

I particularly adore the placement of this line. We've got no idea what 1085 actually is at this point, and this is great foreshadowing. And the final addendum is perfect. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1085's anomalous effects are present in 2█% of the copies

Okay, what possible reason could the SCP foundation have to only censor the second digit? They'd either censor the whole number or none of it. Can you imagine a meeting where someone goes "About the SCP-1085 report, I think we should censor the percentage of tapes affected, but only the units digit. It's okay for the public to know that it's between 20 an 29%, but to know which exact number would be a major safety risk"?

It really feels like it's there for the sake of being there. Just because you put a black box somewhere doesn't make your entry more mysterious, guys.

Come to think of it, it doesn't make much sense for the Foundation to censor the name of the DVD but not the subtitle either as it's specific enough for people to google it and find it. And they'd recognize it in a store or sale as well.

In general, not a very good SCP in my opinion. It's just another "person gets obsessed with SCP until they die" entry and frankly, many of the descriptions are rather silly, especially at the end with the D-Class' reactions which seem straight out of a cheesy low-budget horror movie from the 80's (no matter how hard you try, jumping jacks will never be scary).

I'm guessing it's supposed to be a satire of health gurus and the cult of personality that sometimes surrounds them, or how people can get obsessed with their physical appearance but it's very ham-fisted and feels out of place for an SCP (doesn't help that it's a theme that's been done to death by every form of fiction and it usually comes off as preachy rather than thought-provoking to me).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image in this article was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact anqxyr or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.
It is my honor to bring this article up to a three-digit positive rating. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for nice execution and, especially, the addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Liked it a lot. You could see where it was going but it still played out really well.

Also:

Should the number of SCP-1085 iterations become difficult to contain, alternate measures (mass destruction via steamroller or explosive detonation) may be taken into consideration.

This made me laugh for some reason… I just imagine a Foundation steamroller crushing thousands of DVD's at once.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happened to the robots or does some of the SCP articles takes place in the future? Or you are invoking the selective canon rule?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe I didn't notice the slight cheesiness the first time I read it, because it spoke to some of my greatest fears and scared the ever-loving crap out of me. Upvoted.

Much, much later edit: upon rereading, I realized that the "fitness guru" in question is probably Jillian Michaels, who is scary enough WITHOUT this article D8

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed, cheesy premise solid execution, so I guess that makes it good SCP-camp? I like it. Upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually really don't like the idea of this article - I think it's cheesy and obvious - but the execution is so good (and the tone spot on to boot) that I'm upvoting anyway. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one, simply for the addendum, that did freak me out some what. Upvoted :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First, a D-class used to test an SCP doesn't automatically become an SCP; most to all D-class would be eligible. Second, the likelihood of a D-class being used to destroy this being or becoming a security risk as a result (the principal reason for avoiding cross-SCP testing) is low. Finally, being mentioned in this file sets the precedent to authorizing exactly this kind of operation (not a test). Unless you mean something other than what I'm covering here?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Regarding destruction with soon-to-die D-class: Yeah, the implication is that's how they'll handle it: if they ABSOLUTELY have to destroy some, they'll use a method in which one D-class can destroy many of them at once, so that the effects will be as limited as possible.

But the 'talent pool' for the task is limited to D-class who have not had exposure to any SCPs other than SCP-1085. Rule#1 NO unauthorized cross-SCP testing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's good that you agree… but that doesn't matter. They can be whatever you need them to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree that A = the most powerful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Regarding destruction with soon-to-die D-class: Yeah, the implication is that's how they'll handle it: if they ABSOLUTELY have to destroy some, they'll use a method in which one D-class can destroy many of them at once, so that the effects will be as limited as possible.

Regarding the changing difficulty level: I imagine it would either split the difference or go with the lower common denominator, since if two people of inequal fitness are doing a workout that very nearly kills you, having the workout targeted towards the healthier person will just flat-out kill the other. This early on, the DVD won't seem anomalous, so I doubt it would look different to each member of the audience (since they would look at the other's workout and ask what the hell they were doing).

Regarding someone who isn't overweight: Workout DVDs can also be used for building muscle. I don't know if you're familiar with "manorexia," but in general, body dysmorphic disorder is very, very easy to acquire no matter what your actual physical condition is. Basically, however you look, you can be convinced that you can and should look "better."

Regarding a lack of desire to lose weight: If acting voluntarily, they would stop at week 1. If forced to continue (as in, during testing with D-class), they would begin to want to lose weight in weeks 2 and 3.

Regarding showing the weeks out of order: This was a question I answered in an earlier draft but dropped for this one. Basically, you'd see the empty room, but without the Stockholm-like adoration of the host, it wouldn't affect you as much. Generally, though, I dunno. I would edit to resolve a lot of these questions, but I'm afraid of making the article too bulky. If I were making a full-out experiment log, I'd include these as examples, but I tend not to like either reading or writing experiment logs, so…there we are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subject described an individual with ███████ ████████'s appearance, though with what he described as multiple open wounds on the limbs and a distorted face.

When told that there was nobody else in the room, subject rose from the bed and responded "Funny, that's what she says about you"

Creepy.

Copies of SCP-1085 may be destroyed only by D-class personnel; however, given the doubtful benefit to Foundation research and the total unusability of personnel after such destruction, this is generally considered a waste of resources and requires approval from Site 38 command.

The copies could be destroyed by D-class right before the monthly termination.

Notably, the workout will demonstrate a slight variance depending on the physical ability and willingness of the individual to complete it;

If multiple people are watching the same DVD at the same time, does each person perceive it differently?

Regardless, very few individuals (█%) opt to give up on the program at this point, even when such an option is provided, and even when the individuals did not feel strongly motivated to lose weight at the beginning of the program.

What if a D-class who isn't overweight at all is forced to go through week 1?

Some more questions:

  1. What happens if the SCP is shown to someone who isn't overweight?
  2. What happens if it's shown to someone who is overweight but has no desire to lose weight?
  3. What happens if the weeks are displayed out of order? Specifically, what if someone is shown week 4 without having viewed any of the previous weeks?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done. The concept is very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Decent SCP, OP. I mean, it's not bad, but it isn't your best work either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are frequent canonical disagreements between writers as to the meanings of the different classes of amnesiacs. I always took the side that class A was the most powerful, hence multiple treatments creating a seven-year memory gap. In general, I tend to give people a pass on usage of amnesiacs as long as it's not overblown.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not 'repeated Class A amnesiac treatments' - 'class B' amnesiacs.

or possibly even "Class C".

Upvoted, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I felt like this article fell off, starting with Week 4. I can't really point out what's wrong with the end, but I'm upvoting the rest of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Given that the host takes the form of a "real" person, I'm curious what would happen if the subject was exposed to the original.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is actually the first thing you have done that i havent absolutely loved. but its still a good read and a ton better than some other things on the site, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Many thanks to Godbot and Uncandescent for looking at the draft in-chat.
